productTitle: Ubiquiti airFiber X 5 GHz, 34 dBi, Slant 45
name: Ubiquiti airFiber X 5 GHz, 34 dBi, Slant 45
title: Ubiquiti airFiber X 5 GHz, 34 dBi, Slant 45
short_description: Parabolic Dish Antenna 5GHz 34dBi, Dual Linear Polarization, 2 x RP-SMA Female Connectors
description: The AirFiber 5G34-S45 is a premium sector antenna developed by Ubiquiti Networks. This model is an integral component of Ubiquiti's advanced AirFiber series, renowned for its exceptional capacity to deliver high-performance, long-distance wireless communication solutions. Designed for seamless integration with AirFiber X radios, the AF-5G34-S45 stands as a testament to Ubiquiti's commitment to innovation and excellence in the realm of wireless networking technology.

detailed_description.heading: High-Gain PtP Antenna
detailed_description.text: The AirFiber 5G34-S45 is a premium sector antenna developed by Ubiquiti Networks. This model is an integral component of Ubiquiti's advanced AirFiber series, renowned for its exceptional capacity to deliver high-performance, long-distance wireless communication solutions. Designed for seamless integration with AirFiber X radios, the AF-5G34-S45 stands as a testament to Ubiquiti's commitment to innovation and excellence in the realm of wireless networking technology.

detailed_description.heading: Integration with AirFiber X Radio
detailed_description.text: The AirFiber 5G34-S45 antenna from Ubiquiti is designed to be used in conjunction with an AirFiber X radio from the same brand. It cannot operate on its own as it is a directional antenna that requires a radio unit to transmit and receive signals.

detailed_description.heading: Frequency and Gain Performance
detailed_description.text: Engineered to operate in the 5 GHz frequency band, the AirFiber 5G34-S45 boasts an impressive 34dBi gain, positioning it as a highly capable antenna for establishing point-to-point (PtP) links over extensive distances. This high gain is instrumental in enhancing signal strength and network performance, enabling the deployment of robust wireless backhaul networks that demand minimal interference and maximal throughput. The antenna's focus on the 5 GHz band ensures compatibility with a wide range of wireless applications, from broadband access to backhaul solutions, catering to the diverse needs of modern networks.

detailed_description.heading: Durability and Resilience
detailed_description.text: Durability and resilience are at the core of the AF-5G34-S45's design. Constructed from materials chosen for their robustness, the antenna is built to withstand the harsh realities of outdoor deployment. Weather-resistant properties offer protection against the elements—such as rain, wind, and sun exposure—ensuring the antenna's longevity and consistent performance. This emphasis on durability is crucial for maintaining the integrity of wireless network infrastructure and safeguarding investments in technology over time.

detailed_description.heading: Installation and Design
detailed_description.text: The AirFiber 5G34-S45 is designed with user convenience in mind, featuring a versatile mounting kit that supports straightforward and secure installation on a variety of structures. This flexibility, combined with precision alignment capabilities, allows for optimal positioning to maximize signal coverage and connectivity. The mounting system is complemented by an intuitive design, facilitating easy integration with AirFiber X radios and ensuring a streamlined setup process.

detailed_description.heading: RF Isolation and Slant 45 Technology
detailed_description.text: Advanced RF isolation and noise filtration technologies further distinguish the AF-5G34-S45. These features are essential for operating efficiently in crowded RF environments by minimizing interference and maximizing signal clarity. Superior isolation prevents cross-talk and signal leakage, enhancing the overall performance and reliability of the network. A hallmark of the AF-5G34-S45 is its incorporation of Slant 45 polarization technology. This innovative design employs dual-polarity (both vertical and horizontal polarization) to significantly improve signal penetration, network reliability, and multipath resistance.
